content_en:
Al-Kasani's treatment of taharah in Bada'i as-Sana'i exemplifies the work's distinctive organizational method and analytical clarity. He begins by defining taharah conceptually — as the removal of impurity or its legal equivalent — before proceeding to the specific types: taharah from hadath (ritual impurity through wudu, ghusl, or tayammum) and taharah from khabath (physical impurity through washing and removal).

Al-Kasani's treatment of water begins with the question of why water has this special purifying function. He argues theologically that water is inherently pure and was created with this purifying capacity, citing the Quranic verse 'We made from water every living thing' (21:30) and 'He sent down water from the sky to purify you by it' (8:11). This theological grounding — connecting the legal ruling to the divine wisdom behind it — is characteristic of al-Kasani's approach.

The Hanafi water categories are presented with al-Kasani's systematic method. He identifies three categories: tahur (pure and purifying), tahir (pure but not purifying), and najis (impure). Within tahur, he distinguishes original (mutlaq) water from water whose name has changed. For the middle category (tahir but not tahur), he includes musta'mal water and water that has absorbed pure but name-changing substances. His defense of the musta'mal category — that used water cannot purify because the ritual act of purification has transferred a legal quality to it — is among the clearest statements of this position in Hanafi literature.

For wudu, al-Kasani presents the four fara'id (obligatory acts) with his characteristic attention to the underlying conditions. On the face: the obligation is to wash the entire face — the spatial boundaries of the face are defined vertically from the hairline to the chin and horizontally from earlobe to earlobe. The mouth and nose are within the face and must be rinsed, making madmadah (rinsing the mouth) and istinshaq (rinsing the nose) obligations in the Hanafi school.

The nullifiers of wudu are presented with al-Kasani's organization into categories: physical exits from the body (urine, feces, wind, blood, vomit), loss of consciousness, and specific acts. The question of whether certain foods nullify wudu (the Hanbali position that camel meat does) is discussed, with al-Kasani defending the Hanafi position that no food nullifies wudu — the rule is limited to physical exits from the body.

Ghusl in Bada'i as-Sana'i is organized around its three obligatory acts: madmadah (mouth-rinsing), istinshaq (nose-rinsing), and washing the entire body. Al-Kasani presents the evidential basis for including the mouth and nose as obligatory in ghusl with greater detail than most Hanafi texts, addressing the Shafi'i counter-argument that these are merely recommended. The argument turns on the interpretation of certain hadiths about the Prophet's ghusl and the Quranic command 'purify yourselves' (5:6) as applying to the full body including internal surfaces.
content_id:
Perlakuan al-Kasani terhadap thaharah dalam Bada'i as-Sana'i mencontohkan metode khas karya dan kejelasan analitisnya. Beliau memulai dengan mendefinisikan thaharah secara konseptual — sebagai penghilangan kotoran fisik (khabaits) dan keadaan ritual yang tidak murni (ahdats) — sebelum melanjutkan ke hukum-hukum khusus. Definisi ini bukan sekadar formalitas melainkan menetapkan kerangka analitis yang mengatur seluruh bagian: setiap hukum thaharah dapat dipahami sebagai penerapan salah satu dari dua kategori dasar ini.

Untuk wudhu, al-Kasani mengidentifikasi empat rukun (arkan) — membasuh wajah, membasuh kedua tangan hingga siku, mengusap sebagian kepala, membasuh kedua kaki hingga mata kaki — dan membedakannya dari perbuatan-perbuatan sunnah yang dianjurkan (sunan) dan perbuatan yang dianggap sebagai adab. Ketepatan ini dalam membedakan apa yang wajib dari apa yang dianjurkan adalah salah satu fitur Bada'i yang paling berharga secara praktis bagi para pelajar yang perlu menentukan apakah kelalaian menghasilkan thaharah yang tidak sah atau hanya wudhu yang kurang sempurna.

Perlakuan terhadap mandi wajib (ghusl), tayamum sebagai pengganti wudhu ketika air tidak tersedia, dan penghilangan najis fisik mengikuti pola yang sama: definisi yang jelas, rukun-rukun esensial, syarat-syarat kesahan, faktor-faktor pembatal, dan pertanyaan-pertanyaan cabang. Metode ini memudahkan perbandingan antara tipe-tipe thaharah yang berbeda dan membantu pelajar memahami struktur logis hukum thaharah Hanafi.
